statement.p: A school had a fund-raising event. The revenue came from three resources: ticket sales, auction sales, and donations. Ticket sales account for {{\frac{1}{5}}} of the total revenue; auction sales account for {{\frac{7}{10}}} of the total revenue. What fraction of the revenue came from donations?
of the revenue came from donations.

solution.p: The revenue consists of 3 parts. The total revenue is like a big 1; ticket sales account for {{\frac{1}{5}}}; auction sales account for {{\frac{7}{10}}}. To find the missing piece (from donations), we use subtraction.
Note that a common denominator of 1, {{\frac{1}{5}}} and {{\frac{7}{10}}} is 10.
So {{\frac{1}{10}}} of the revenue came from donations.
